msgs.text:
I read the problem statement and the prior-turn log as external proposals. The prompt fixes the four-generator tame format with distinguished sigma,tau, implicit tau^sigma = tau^2, later generators in the pro-2 core, and exponents reduced modulo M=85667662080.The prior log leaves only the ‚Äúdynamic tame-inertia‚Äù candidate alive; the earlier scalar, plain-œÄ2, and fixed-œÑ‚àí2 candidates were refuted there by explicit finite-tame or index-two tests.

The live candidate remains:variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently, with œÄ=œÄ2 and Œ∑=2œÄ on odd tame inertia,r=xœÉ‚àíœÄxœÉœÄx‚àí1(xœÑ‚àíŒ∑)œÄ ((yœÑ)œÄ)œÉy‚àí1,where œÄ‚â°40491355905,‚àíœÄ‚â°45176306175,‚àíŒ∑‚â°50932630783(modM).The 2-primary coordinate of Œ∑ is irrelevant in this occurrence, because it is used only as an exponent of the tame inertia generator œÑ, whose finite images have odd order.The first point of this turn is to settle the reciprocal-formula issue that has oscillated in the last several prior turns. For the sign-cover calculation, let t be the deck involution of V=kerŒ∫, and work on a nontrivial tame-inertia character block where œÑ‚Ü¶Œ±ÓÄ†=1. ForNa (u)=(uœÑa)œÄ,the Fox coefficient is the completed geometric sumDa=j=0‚àëœÄ‚àí1(tŒ±a)j.

The correct identity isDa=1‚àítŒ±a 1‚àí(tŒ±a)œÄ=1‚àítŒ±a1‚àít,because œÄ‚â°1(mod2) and œÄ‚â°0(mode), so (tŒ±a)œÄ=t. This is the direction that makes the unit/radical argument work: 1‚àítŒ±a is a unit for Œ±aÓÄ†=1, while 1‚àít lies in the radical of the 2-adic sign-cover group algebra. Hence Da is radical, and the coefficient fromx‚àí1(xœÑ‚àíŒ∑)œÄ is ‚àí1+D‚àíŒ∑, a unit. Thus nontrivial odd tame-inertia character blocks contribute no 2-primary Smith torsion.The second recurring formula is the trivial tame-inertia block. WithA=F2 [T]/(Tf‚àí1),s‚â°œÄ(modf),the linear relation is(1+T‚àís)X+(1+T)Y=0.

If f=2a m, m odd, then s‚â°1(mod2a) and s‚â°0(modm). For even f, s is odd, so 1+T‚à£1+T‚àís. Therefore the legitimate substitution is

Y=P(T)X+YÀâ,P(T)=1+T1+T‚àís.Then h(T)=1+P(T)+P(T‚àí1),and for a nontrivial f-th root Œ∂,(1+Œ∂)h(Œ∂)=Œ∂‚àís+Œ∂s+1.

So h(Œ∂)=0 would force Œ∂2s+1=1. But 2s+1 is odd and congruent to 1(modm), hence gcd(f,2s+1)=1. Therefore the trivial block remains nonsingular. The correct pair of formulas is thereforeDa=1‚àítŒ±a1‚àít,P(T)=1+T1+T‚àís.Using these corrected formulas, the prior abelian checks remain valid:Ue,fab‚âÖZ2ef+1‚äïC2,and for the sign kernel,(kerŒ∫)ab‚âÖZ22ef+1‚äïC4.My new computation is a refinement of Turn 36‚Äôs mixed even-f ambiguity calculation. Turn 36 correctly found that in the first mixed case (e,f)=(3,2), after killing the odd Hall ambiguity, the first marked IA deformation space has dimension 5, compared with dimension 3 in the pure unramified quadratic case. I independently reconstructed that calculation and extracted an explicit basis.LetŒî3,2‚âÉS3=‚ü®œÑ,œÉ‚à£œÑ3=œÉ2=1,¬†œÉœÑœÉ‚àí1=œÑ2‚ü©,with H=‚ü®œÑ‚ü©‚âÉC3 and P=‚ü®œÉ‚ü©‚âÉC2. In degree one,L1‚âÖF2[S3]‚äïF2 b‚äïF2S,where S=œÉ2 is marked and fixed. WriteAi=XœÑi,Bi=XœÑiœÉ (i‚ààZ/3).

The quadratic initial relation isq=i‚ààZ/3‚àë(Ai[2]+Bi[2])+[b,S].

The degree-two Lie piece isL2=(L1[2]‚äï‚àß2L1)/‚ü®q‚ü©,of dimension 35. The marked first IA ambiguity isH1(C2,HomC3(F2[S3]‚äïF2b,L2)),and direct F2 -linear reduction gives dimker(1+œÉ)=44,rank(1+œÉ)=39,sodimH1=44‚àí39=5.

The structural simplification is this: the regular F2[S3]-part contributes no cohomology by the induced-module argument. All five classes come from maps b‚Ü¶w, where w‚ààL2H is P-fixed modulo (1+P)L2H. In fact,L2H‚âÖR4‚äïT5 as an F2[C2]-module, where R is the two-dimensional regular indecomposable and T is the trivial module. The five trivial representatives may be chosen asb[2],S[2],Z0,Z1,Z2,whereZd=i‚ààZ/3‚àë[Ai,Bi+d](d=0,1,2).

The class Z0+Z1+Z2 is the unramified-looking cross-commutator direction. The two augmentation-zero combinations among Z0,Z1,Z2, for exampleZ0+Z1,Z0+Z2,are genuinely mixed tame directions absent in the unramified quadratic case.There is also a useful relation-preserving correction for these mixed directions. If one begins withŒ¥d (b)=Zd,Œ¥d (S)=0,then the derivative of q contributes [Zd,S]. This can be cancelled canonically by settingŒ¥d(Ai)=[Bi+d,S],Œ¥d(Bi+d)=[Ai,S](i‚ààZ/3).

Indeed, summing over i and using Jacobi in characteristic 2,i‚àë([Ai,[Bi+d,S]]+[Bi+d,[Ai,S]])=[Zd,S],so the contribution from the X-squares cancels the contribution from [b,S]. Thus the mixed Zd -directions are not merely raw module-cohomology artifacts; they have natural first relation-preserving lifts. I am not claiming here that these tangent directions integrate to independent profinite actions without higher corrections, but they are genuine low-level filtered-action directions that a final formation theorem must control.This sharpens the remaining obstruction. Odd Hall rigidity removes the prime-to-2 tame ambiguity, and the abelianization/cup/orientation checks now look internally consistent for the live word. But in even-f mixed tame quotients, the residual C2a -Frobenius action has extra H-fixed IA directions. In the first mixed case (3,2), the two augmentation-zero combinations of Z0,Z1,Z2 are precisely the first visible mixed-tame directions not detected by pure unramified tests, abelianization, sign-kernel Smith forms, or the basic Demu≈°kin orientation invariant.

ESTABLISHED: The live dynamic candidate remains unrefuted. The correct reciprocal formulas areDa=1‚àítŒ±a1‚àít,P(T)=1+T1+T‚àís.

With these, the prior conclusions Ue,fab‚âÖZ2ef+1‚äïC2 and (kerŒ∫)ab‚âÖZ22ef+1‚äïC4 survive. New this turn: in the mixed quotient (e,f)=(3,2), the first marked IA ambiguity space has an explicit five-class basis represented by b[2],S[2],Z0,Z1,Z2, with the two augmentation-zero combinations among the Zd giving genuinely mixed tame directions. The Zd have canonical first relation-preserving corrections.

GAP: The candidate is still not a proved presentation of GQ2. The missing step is a dyadic formation/rigidity theorem identifying the candidate‚Äôs exact filtered even-Frobenius action with arithmetic. Mixed even-f tame quotients have extra filtered-action directions beyond the pure unramified case.

NEXT: Compute the candidate‚Äôs actual coefficients on the two mixed augmentation-zero classes Z0+Z1 and Z0+Z2 in the (e,f)=(3,2) quotient, then compare those coefficients with explicit Kummer/Witt arithmetic through U/U5.[BREAKTHROUGH]
